etymology.description: semantic 頁(頁) (yè, "head") + phonetic 令(令) (lìng, "order") -> lead; usher; take
etymology.interpretation: Semantic 頁(頁) (yè, "head") suggests direction or leadership, as the head leads the body. Phonetic 令(令) (lìng, "order") provides the sound, enhancing the concept of directing or leading, where orders are followed. The combination reflects the idea of guiding or taking the lead, akin to a head giving direction to an order.
etymology.simplification: 領(領) was simplified by reducing 頁(頁) to 页(頁), decreasing stroke count without altering phonetic 令(令).

examples.chinese: 他带领我们去参观博物馆(他帶領我們去參觀博物館)。
examples.pinyin: Tā dàilǐng wǒmen qù cānguān bówùguǎn.
examples.english: He led us to visit the museum.

examples.chinese: 她在课堂上总是能领会到老师的意思(她在課堂上總是能領會到老師的意思)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài kètáng shàng zǒng shì néng lǐnghuì dào lǎoshī de yìsi.
examples.english: She always grasps the teacher's meaning in class.

examples.chinese: 经过他的领导(經過他的領導)，团队取得了巨大进步(團隊取得了巨大進步)。
examples.pinyin: Jīngguò tā de lǐngdǎo, tuánduì qǔdéle jùdà jìnbù.
examples.english: Under his leadership, the team made significant progress.
